attacking_style: Rarely attacks independently; primarily serves as final climb lead-out man for team leaders. When given GC freedom (Paris-Nice 2023), can finish strong but doesn't initiate decisive accelerations.
team_tactics: Deployed as high-tempo domestique on final climbs, controlling pace rather than attacking. INEOS uses him for breakaway control and sustained climbing support, not as explosive aggressor.
terrain_preference: Sustained steep climbs where tempo control matters more than explosive accelerations. Struggles on technical descents and shows vulnerability in nervous peloton conditions on flatter stages.
vulnerability: Positioning fragility in chaotic stages (crash losses 13+ minutes). Cannot match aggressive racing when leaders attack early. Mechanical issues require chase efforts that compromise GC. Descending concentration lapses under fatigue.
key_patterns: Crashes/mechanicals derail GC campaigns: 13-minute losses (Giro 2021), chase efforts compromise defense (Giro 2022).
Dropped early when leaders ride aggressively: unable to support Bernal's attacks (Vuelta 2021), 5:40 down week one (Vuelta 2022).
Functions as tempo domestique not accelerator: led Carapaz without attacking Giro 2022, worked for breakaway control Paris-Nice 2023.
Best results when given protected GC role rather than pure domestique duties: 7th Paris-Nice when freed.
Technical/concentration errors under fatigue: unforced descent crash Dauphiné 2020, positioning crashes sprint stages.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Paris-Nice 7th shows solid climbing form, but still in domestique role. Pattern of early GT elimination (2021-2022) suggests Grand Tour resilience remains unproven when given leadership.
